title: Support Ticket #4821 - Zepto UPI Failure During Sale
text: Ticket #4821. Client: Zepto. Priority: P1. Date: March 15, 2024. Issue: UPI transactions failing at 22% rate between 8 PM - 10 PM during their weekend sale event. Normal failure rate is 2%. Impact: Estimated ₹4.2 crore in lost GMV. Resolution: Root cause identified as NPCI throttling during peak hour. Razorpay smart routing was not configured for Zepto's account. Fix: Enabled multi-bank UPI routing (HDFC + Kotak + ICICI fallback). Failure rate dropped to 1.8% post-fix. Time to resolve: 3.5 hours. Compensation offered: ₹50,000 credit on next invoice. Follow-up: Capacity planning meeting scheduled before next sale event.

title: Support Ticket #3917 - Juspay Settlement Delay
text: Ticket #3917. Client: Juspay India. Priority: P2. Date: February 3, 2024. Issue: Settlement delay of 3 additional business days beyond contracted T+2. Affected ₹28 crore in settlements. Root cause: Banking partner (Yes Bank) processing queue backlog during their internal system migration. Impact: Juspay had to arrange bridge financing, incurring ₹18 lakh in interest costs. Resolution: Settlement processed manually via alternate banking partner. Issue resolved in 5 days total. Compensation: Offered ₹12 lakh credit — Juspay rejected, demanding full ₹18 lakh reimbursement. Status: Escalated to VP Sales. Unresolved. This is the primary reason for Juspay's low health score of 68/100. Action required: Legal to review contract SLA clause. Finance to assess compensation exposure.

title: Support Ticket #5102 - Groww NACH Rejection Rate
text: Ticket #5102. Client: Groww. Priority: P2. Date: April 2, 2024. Issue: NACH (National Automated Clearing House) mandate rejections at 6% vs industry average of 3.5%. Affects SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) auto-debit. Impact: Each rejected mandate = failed SIP = user drops SIP = Groww loses recurring investment user. At 95 lakh transactions/month, 6% rejection = 5.7 lakh failed SIPs monthly. Customer impact is significant. Root cause analysis: Banks rejecting mandates due to account name mismatch (full name vs short name in NACH database). Razorpay NACH does not do pre-validation of account holder name before mandate creation. Fix proposed: Add account name fuzzy-matching pre-check before NACH registration. Implementation timeline: 6 weeks (engineering). Interim workaround: Add retry logic with alternative account details prompt. Groww engineering team engaged directly. Weekly sync scheduled.

title: Support Ticket #4455 - Dunzo MDR Cost Complaint
text: Ticket #4455. Client: Dunzo Daily. Priority: P3 (commercial). Date: March 8, 2024. Issue: Not a technical issue. Commercial complaint — Dunzo's Finance team escalated that their Stripe MDR at 2.9% is significantly higher than what competitors are paying on Razorpay. They have received a proposal from Razorpay offering 1.85% for their volume. Financial impact: Dunzo processes ₹420 crore annually. At Stripe's 2.9% = ₹12.18 crore annual processing cost. At Razorpay's 1.85% = ₹7.77 crore. Difference = ₹4.41 crore per year. Account Manager note: Dunzo is on Stripe due to a 2021 decision by their previous CTO who preferred Stripe's developer experience. Current tech team does not have strong preference. This is a commercial issue. Action: Rahul Nair to present retention proposal by March 25. Status: Proposal submitted, awaiting Dunzo decision. Renewal due September 2024 — this is critical.

title: Support Ticket #5298 - BrowserStack Indian Card Failures
text: Ticket #5298. Client: BrowserStack. Priority: P2. Date: April 18, 2024. Issue: 23% of Indian credit card transactions failing on Stripe due to RBI 2FA (two-factor authentication) requirement. Stripe's 3DS implementation has compatibility issues with some Indian bank OTP flows. Impact: BrowserStack estimates ₹8 crore in lost annual India revenue from declined cards. Root cause: Stripe processes Indian cards through international routing which hits additional authentication layers. Resolution path: BrowserStack to add Razorpay for India-origin customers only. Stripe continues for international. This is the split-processor strategy. Implementation plan: BrowserStack dev team to implement geo-based processor routing (India IP → Razorpay, Others → Stripe). Timeline: 4 weeks. Expected outcome: Indian card success rate improves from 77% to 96%. Estimated revenue recovery: ₹6-7 crore annually.
